name: Jay Mercer
title: Space Cowboys, Inc. (founder, sole employee until further notice)

missions.name: Near-Earth Pickup
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Got a buyer wanting raw ore off a rock close to home, no paperwork, no drama. Land on the surface, pop the cargo bay with F, and shovel until the hold reads its number. Lift off too early and the whole run pays nothing — buyer wants the full quote or nothing. Easy money for a clean burn home. Coffee fund stuff. — Jay

missions.name: Belt Run — Full Hold
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Bigger contract this one. Rock is deeper into the belt and the buyer is paying for weight, not speed. Land clean, F to crack the cargo bay, and do not lift off until the hold gauge tops out — partial loads bounce on this paperwork. Honest work for honest credits. Try not to crunch the gear on the deceleration burn, I am not the one signing the repair slip. — Jay

missions.name: Probe Drop
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Science crew at the institute wants gravimetric reads off the surface and they want them yesterday. Land near the marker, plant the probes on foot, and beat the timer — the readings only count if they settle while you are still on the rock. Habitat is H if you need a minute between drops. Pays decent for a guy with patient boots. — Jay

missions.name: Tag and Sample
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Buyer wants composition before they pay haul rates — meaning no sample, no number, no credits. Land, find the field terminal, and tag rocks with the SCI gun. Terminal calls out the keeper. Bring that one home. Paperwork with a drill bit, but it pays better than honest digging. — Jay

missions.name: Jovian Pickup
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Cowboys board has a Jupiter slot now and the margins are better than the inner runs — which, honestly, was not a tall fence to clear. Same job as ever: land on the rock, F to open the bay, fill the hold, fly home. Slingshot windows out here are honest once you learn to read them. Mind the radiation lines on approach. — Jay

missions.name: Jovian Listing
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Another Jovian listing dropped on the board this week — same gather job, the rate is just up. Nobody at the desk can tell me why and I have learned not to ask twice when the number is good. Hold full, F to seal the bay, burn home. Easy day if the weather holds. — Jay

missions.name: Jovian Probe Sweep
missions.briefing: Hey, you got Jay. Survey work this side of the system this time — gravimetric probes on a Jovian rock before the timer runs out. Same job, new neighborhood. Land near the marker, plant the probes on foot, then bring the data home. Slingshot windows out here actually run on time once you learn the rhythm. H if you need a sandwich between drops. — Jay
